title: My Feelings: K-2 Sentence Frames (English-Spanish)
description: A set of simple sentence frames designed for K-2 ELL newcomer students to express basic feelings in English and Spanish, promoting classroom communication.

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a picture of how you feel today. Then complete the sentence below.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja cómo te sientes hoy. Luego completa la oración de abajo.

items.correct_answer: Student's choice of feeling word and drawing.

worksheets.instructions_en: Look at the picture. Complete the sentence with the correct feeling word. (Word bank: happy, sad, mad, tired)
worksheets.instructions_l2: Mira la imagen. Completa la oración con la palabra correcta para la emoción. (Banco de palabras: feliz, triste, enojado/a, cansado/a)

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and express basic feelings (happy, sad, mad, scared, excited, tired, hungry, shy) using simple 3-5 word sentence frames in English and Spanish.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce one or two frames daily. Use feelings flashcards for visual support. Model sentences repeatedly. Incorporate into daily 'check-in' routines or morning meetings. Encourage students to use frames during social interactions. Role-play different scenarios where feelings might be expressed.
materials_needed: Feelings flashcards (pictures of faces expressing emotions)
Student journals or blank paper for drawing/writing
Printouts of dialogue strips and worksheets
differentiation_tips: For emergent learners: Provide only 2-3 choices for fill-in-the-blank. Allow pointing to pictures instead of speaking. Focus on 'I am happy' and 'I feel sad' first.
For developing learners: Encourage full sentence responses. Pair with a more proficient peer for dialogue practice. Use the frames to describe characters in stories.
For advanced learners: Encourage adding 'because...' to explain feelings (e.g., 'I am happy because...'). Introduce more complex feeling words as appropriate.
